div.p: Neither method advances the cursor. Both are O(1) reads.
Cursor invariant: Neither peek() nor _peek_next() calls advance(). self.current is unchanged after both calls. Only advance() moves the cursor.

Why peek() alone is insufficient: Row 2 (3.foo) shows that peek() == '.' is TRUE yet we must NOT enter float mode. Only _peek_next() reveals the following char is 'f' (not a digit). Without the second lookahead, the scanner would consume '.' and emit NUMBER("3.") — an invalid token requiring backtracking to fix. Two-character lookahead eliminates backtracking entirely.
